name: secrets-of-power-negotiating-inside-secrets-from-a-master-negotiator
description: Roger Dawson's Secrets of Power Negotiating — an executable toolkit that translates any negotiation situation into the Gambits framework (Beginning → Middle → Ending) and applies pressure points, power dynamics, and the Power Negotiator's Creed to get the best deal while leaving the other side feeling they won.
useCase: Real-world negotiation skills for sales, purchasing, salary negotiation, real estate, business deals, and conflict resolution — covering 50+ Gambits, 10 power sources, and 3-tier conflict resolution (impasse/stalemate/deadlock).
knownRisks: Some Gambits (Decoy, Deliberate Mistake, Escalation) are unethical — the AI should flag them as such before teaching.
Good Guy/Bad Guy and Higher Authority can damage trust if overused or detected.
Cross-cultural differences: Gambits that work in American culture may backfire in high-context cultures (Japan, Middle East).
The Flinch technique can offend if done theatrically — the AI should teach it as a subtle reaction, not a performance.
